title: Archived workspace omits data/kaggle/, so 18/24 tests fail on re-score
evidence: .pytest_cache/v/cache/lastfailed lists 18 failing tests (all engine/datasets-fixture tests); data/kaggle/ absent from run_dir; scores.json shows test_coverage=0.3 / defect_rate=0.0 vs authoritative retort.db completed row test_coverage=0.85 / defect_rate=0.68 (finished 2026-04-13)
suggestion: Archive the input CSVs (or a fixture subset) with the run, or have conftest skip data-dependent tests when SOCCER_DATA_DIR is unavailable, so the run remains verifiable after archival

title: Tests are comprehensive but not runnable in the archived workspace
evidence: 24 tests across 5 files (tests/test_*.py); original run scored test_coverage=0.85 in retort.db, but the archived copy cannot execute the 18 data-dependent tests because data/kaggle/ is missing
suggestion: Ship data fixtures with the archive so test_coverage reproduces

title: Dead partial-match loop in normalize_team does nothing
evidence: soccer_mcp/data_loader.py:66-70 iterates TEAM_ALIASES but every branch is `pass`; the loop has no effect
suggestion: Remove the dead loop or implement the intended long-name partial match

title: Tests use plain pytest with Scenario docstrings, not gherkin BDD
evidence: TASK.md:348-365 suggests gherkin/BDD scenarios; tests/test_matches.py uses `"""Scenario: ..."""` docstrings on pytest functions rather than a behave/pytest-bdd feature file
suggestion: Optional — adopt pytest-bdd if strict gherkin conformance is desired; current style is functionally equivalent
